dataSourceNote: This session's network egress policy blocked the normal data pipeline for this run: direct requests to query1.finance.yahoo.com, news.google.com, and www.sec.gov all returned policy-level 403s at the proxy (confirmed via the proxy status endpoint's recentRelayFailures, kind: connect_rejected). A retry via the WebFetch tool (which does not route through this session's local proxy) also got a 403 directly from Yahoo Finance, indicating Yahoo itself is blocking that request path too. As a fallback, current price/news context below was gathered via WebSearch instead of scripts/fetch-market-data.mjs + fetch-news.mjs + fetch-fundamentals.mjs. This is qualitative, third-party-summarized data, not the structured OHLCV/technical-indicator/EDGAR feed used on other decision days, and it is noticeably noisier: e.g. NVDA's 'current' price was restated two different ways ($211.80 vs $207.57) within a single search result, and MSFT's cited 52-week high ($555.45) is inconsistent with its ~$385 quoted price in the same summary. No BUY/SELL/SHORT/COVER decision was sized off this data as a result — see reasoning in data/decisions.json for the full risk-assessment rationale.

webSearchFindings.AAPL: Record high ~$321.29 on 2026-07-13, +16% since 2026-06-25. Catalysts: Broadcom chip supply deal extension through 2031 (announced 2026-07-06), on-device AI progress, CEO transition from Tim Cook to John Ternus, Citi price target raised to $365. Valuation ~34x forward earnings vs 10-yr avg 23x. Analyst consensus Moderate Buy, avg target $327.20 (~3.4% upside from record high). Earnings due 2026-07-30. Sources: tipranks.com, gurufocus.com, fxleaders.com, stockinvest.us (via WebSearch).

decisionProcess: Applied the system prompt's bull/bear/risk-manager framework across the watchlist using the qualitative web-search findings above in place of the unreachable structured pipeline. AAPL, NVDA, JPM, and AVGO all show real bullish catalysts, but each has already made its move (record high, post-earnings pop, or deal-news pop) rather than offering a fresh, well-timed entry; AMZN and META carry a real fundamental headwind (heavy guided AI capex pressuring near-term FCF). None of the candidates combined a sound entry with data precise enough to compute a defensible Entry Price / Stop Loss / Take Profit / Risk-Reward Ratio, so the risk-manager check failed for all of them and every candidate was downgraded to HOLD.
